title: Messaging Outcomes and Delivery Evidence in Go · Mastery Check
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for messaging outcomes and delivery evidence in go and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a typed outcome and ownership evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define producer, consumer, operator, security, accessibility, latency, loss, duplicate, and recovery evidence for one messaging outcome; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Distinguish commands, events, notifications, documents, and queries by ownership, audience, expected handling, and change semantics; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

State at-most-once, at-least-once, effectively-once effect, ordering, and durability claims without promising impossible end-to-end exactly-once delivery; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Transfer Evidence Plan
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 7757 during outbox relay recovery after a database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate local publication, broker custody, and observable stakeholder effect; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement owner; then cancel the parent context while a result is still ambiguous. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Plan browser models, Go compilation, vet, table tests, fuzzing, race and leak checks, disposable RabbitMQ integration, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production transfer gates.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Transfer Evidence Plan.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for plan browser models, go compilation, vet, table tests, fuzzing, race and leak checks, disposable rabbitmq integration, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production transfer gates. For RabbitMQ and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Transfer Evidence Plan, keep Transfer Evidence Plan connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Transfer Evidence Plan, keep Transfer Evidence Plan connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Plan browser models, Go compilation, vet, table tests, fuzzing, race and leak checks, disposable RabbitMQ integration, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production transfer gates.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Transfer Evidence Plan, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Transfer Evidence Plan precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Transfer Evidence Plan, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A quiz Go messaging team handles case 7948 during race-enabled integration test with duplicate delivery. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate local publication, broker custody, and observable stakeholder effect; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Plan browser models, Go compilation, vet, table tests, fuzzing, race and leak checks, disposable RabbitMQ integration, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production transfer gates.

For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Transfer Evidence Plan, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A deterministic browser model or ordinary go test run proves broker replication, goroutine scheduling, and network recovery behavior.
